next_steps: Send cerebrospinal fluid for cytology, flow cytometry, and PARR analysis
Perform EBV and JCV PCR testing on CSF
Obtain whole-body PET/CT to evaluate for systemic lymphoproliferative disorders
Obtain expert neuropathology consultation including immunohistochemistry for markers like CD20, CD3, and EBER
Consider repeat stereotactic biopsy with neuronavigation if initial biopsy non-diagnostic
Continue high-dose corticosteroids only if clinical suspicion for lymphoma remains high and imminent herniation is a concern
Consult infectious disease for consideration of opportunistic infections given long-term immunosuppression

differential_diagnoses: Infectious cerebritis (e.g., Nocardia, Aspergillus, or Toxoplasmosis)
next_steps: Perform urgent expert neuropathology review with immunohistochemistry (CD20, CD3, EBER, GFAP, Ki-67, and silver/acid-fast stains)
Serum toxoplasmosis IgG/IgM and Cryptococcus antigen testing
Repeat MRI with susceptibility-weighted imaging to differentiate hemorrhage from tumor progression
Monitor clinical response to high-dose dexamethasone (the 'steroid test') while awaiting definitive tissue diagnosis
Evaluate for systemic lymphoproliferative disease with body PET/CT or CT chest/abdomen/pelvis
Consult infectious disease for possible escalation to broad-spectrum empiric antimicrobial coverage for atypical pathogens
